skills.name: read-single-lead-signal
skills.description: Read a single ECG lead waveform using the documented ECG API before computing lead-local evidence.
skills.procedure_summary: Call `ecg.get_lead_signals(lead_name)` and limit subsequent analysis to one lead-level claim at a time.
skills.applicability: Use when a feature can be measured directly from one lead waveform.
failure_modes: Waveform is too noisy for reliable numeric measurement
reflection_notes: Prediction did not match the gold answer. The current task should produce corrective feedback so the reused skills do not become over-trusted. The task may have leaned too heavily on tool-routed morphology evidence or interpreted tool evidence too strongly. VLM slice audit was valid, so the failure should not be attributed to segment slicing without additional evidence.
Prediction matched the gold answer. Reusable skills that contributed to this task should be reinforced and reused on similar questions. Correct outcome with task-grounded evidence.
skills.last_reflection: Prediction matched the gold answer. Reusable skills that contributed to this task should be reinforced and reused on similar questions. Correct outcome with task-grounded evidence.

skills.name: extract-ecg-segment-window
skills.description: Use segment boundaries to isolate a waveform or interval before measurement.
skills.procedure_summary: Call `ecg.get_lead_segment(lead_name, segment_name)` and derive measurements from one returned `(start, end)` window.
skills.applicability: Use when a feature depends on a known ECG segment such as QRS, QT, T wave, or PR.

skills.name: convert-segment-index-to-duration
skills.description: Convert ECG sample indices into time duration only when sampling frequency is verified.
skills.procedure_summary: Compute duration with `(end - start) * 1000 / fs` after confirming that `fs` is available from documented context.
skills.applicability: Use for QT, PR, QRS, RR, or similar duration-style questions.
failure_modes: Sampling frequency is missing or undocumented

skills.name: route-morphology-checks-to-visual-tool
skills.description: Route visually defined or noise-sensitive morphology claims to the ECG figure analysis tool.
input_requirements: verified fs for plotting when required
skills.procedure_summary: When the feature is mainly visual or fragile under simple numeric rules, call `ECG_fig_anaysis_tool` and treat the result as supporting evidence.
skills.applicability: Use for scooped ST, subtle contour change, or similar morphology-heavy evidence.
failure_modes: Tool output is treated as direct ground truth
Too many cycles are passed to the visual tool at once

skills.name: map-validated-feature-evidence-to-choice
skills.description: Map validated feature evidence back to the allowed answer choices instead of producing free-form diagnoses.
skills.procedure_summary: Compare only validated evidence against the provided answer options and select the matching option names explicitly.
skills.applicability: Use for multi-choice ECGQA tasks where answers must come from a fixed option list.
failure_modes: Evidence is weaker than the claimed answer
The response contains diagnoses outside the provided options

skills.name: refuse-duration-claim-without-fs
skills.description: Avoid duration-based conclusions when the task does not provide a verified sampling frequency.
skills.procedure_summary: Before computing any duration, verify `fs` from documented context; otherwise mark the duration claim unverified instead of hard-coding a value.
skills.applicability: Use whenever durations in ms are required but sampling frequency is not documented.
failure_modes: Hidden assumptions about `fs` slip into code
Undocumented ECG attributes are treated as guaranteed
reflection_notes: Prediction matched the gold answer. Reusable skills that contributed to this task should be reinforced and reused on similar questions. Correct outcome with task-grounded evidence.
